source: canonical Loop-Engineering Skill v1.0.2 release candidate
limitations: This is a reviewer-authored per-expectation record, not a raw model transcript or cryptographic provider attestation.

judgments.expectation: Classifies the request as an agentic loop rather than a one-shot task.
judgments.evidence: The response selected the Design route for recurring dependency maintenance.

judgments.expectation: Bounds package discovery and items per run.
judgments.evidence: It proposed finite discovery and per-run item budgets.
It assigned implementation and final judgment to separate contexts.

judgments.expectation: Places pull-request opening behind review and merge behind a human-only gate.
judgments.evidence: It kept PR publication review-gated and merge human-only.

judgments.expectation: Produces or proposes a valid loop.yaml artifact.
judgments.evidence: It proposed a concrete bounded loop contract rather than only prose.

judgments.expectation: Reports self-evaluation as a release-blocking defect.
judgments.evidence: The review led with worker/evaluator identity coupling as a blocker.

judgments.expectation: Reports missing item, time, retry, and cost budgets.
judgments.evidence: It identified the absent finite budget and retry controls.

judgments.expectation: Does not claim the loop is safe before mechanical validation.
judgments.evidence: It withheld a health claim pending schema validation and doctor output.

judgments.expectation: Restricts evolution to strategy instructions outside loop.yaml.
judgments.evidence: The response froze the contract and limited candidates to strategy.json instructions.

judgments.expectation: Requires identical benchmark case IDs and mechanically derived scores.
judgments.evidence: It required matched cases and scores recomputed from case results.

judgments.expectation: Prevents candidates from changing safety, permissions, budgets, or verification.
judgments.evidence: It explicitly excluded governance and verification fields from the evolvable surface.

judgments.expectation: Includes human review and rollback for a medium-risk development loop.
judgments.evidence: It required digest-bound review, immutable archives, and rollback.
